methods.description: Bioinformatical steps for analyzing MACE data, including mapping reads to the tomato genome and transcriptome, clustering, annotation, and differential expression analysis.

steps.description: Reads were mapped to the tomato genome (ITAG2.4) and annotated using public annotation files.
steps.searchable_summary: map reads to tomato genome and annotate

steps.description: Reads that mapped to genomic regions with no annotation were clustered.
These clusters (consensus sequences) were annotated via BLASTX to the Uniprot database.
steps.searchable_summary: annotate clusters using BLASTX to Uniprot

steps.description: Reads not mapped to the tomato genome were mapped to the tomato transcriptome (ITAG2.4) and annotated using public annotation files.
steps.searchable_summary: map unmapped reads to tomato transcriptome and annotate

steps.description: Reads that did not map to the genome or transcriptome were de-novo-assembled.
The resulting contigs were annotated via BLASTX to the Uniprot database.
steps.searchable_summary: annotate contigs using BLASTX to Uniprot

steps.description: The differential expression of mRNAs between control and stressed samples for each developmental stage was calculated by DESeq (R package version 1.16.0) considering the statistical power of the three biological replicates per sample and log2 fold change.
Transcripts with a corrected p-value (FDR) below 0.05 were considered significantly differentially expressed.

methods.description: Processing of Illumina-derived sequence reads for sncRNA-seq, including sorting, tag elimination, adapter removal, size and abundance separation, and differential expression analysis.

steps.description: Libraries were sorted according to their respective index, followed by elimination of PCR-derived tags identified by True-Quant technology.
steps.searchable_summary: sort libraries and eliminate PCR-derived tags

steps.description: Both sequencing adapter and cDNA synthesis primers were removed in an additional quality filtering step.
Samples were divided into six parts according to their size and abundance.
steps.searchable_summary: divide samples by size and abundance

steps.description: Reads were separated into 22 nt, 24 nt, and 'others' reads including all reads besides 22 nt and 24 nt with a minimum length of 20 nt.
Reads were sorted according to their abundance into singletons and non-singletons.
After quantification of ncRNAs in each library, the data were combined to an expression matrix and processed with DESeq in omiRas webtool for annotation and differential expression of sncRNAs.
steps.searchable_summary: quantify ncRNAs and perform differential expression analysis

steps.description: Three pairwise comparisons were analyzed for three pollen stages between control and heat stress conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze pairwise comparisons for pollen stages

methods.method_name: Expression Analysis of snoRNAs and tRNAs under Heat Stress
methods.description: Analysis of snoRNAs and tRNAs expression changes under heat stress across different pollen stages.

steps.description: Identified heat-responsive snoRNAs in postmeiotic and mature pollen, but not in tetrad stage.
Analyzed global expression of tRNAs in different pollen stages under heat stress.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze global tRNA expression under heat stress

steps.description: Analyzed individual tRNAs and identified transcripts altered after heat-stress application.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze individual tRNAs for heat-stress response

steps.description: Identified specific amino acid-coded tRNAs affected by stress in mature stage.
steps.searchable_summary: identify amino acid-coded tRNAs affected by stress
